metadata.title: KDE Connect Comprehensive Research
metadata.description: Complete research on KDE Connect architecture, configuration, API usage, plugins, and best practices

overview.description: KDE Connect is a modular, UI-agnostic system for secure wireless communication between devices
design_principles: UI-agnostic core library with DBus interfaces
Secure communication using TLS 1.2 and SFTP
Cross-platform support (Linux, Android, iOS, Windows, macOS)
overview.protocol: Custom secure protocol based on JSON NetworkPackets

link_providers.description: Manage device discovery and network connections

network_packets.description: JSON-serializable data structures for inter-device communication

plugins.extensibility: Easy to add new plugins without changing core protocol

purpose: UI elements interact with core daemon
No direct network handling in UI

encryption.certificate_generation: Self-signed certificates with UUID as common name

authentication.trust_model: Similar to SSH, verify certificate on first use

verification.list_installed: dnf list installed kde* | grep connect

install_dependencies: sudo dnf install extra-cmake-modules kf5-kcmutils-devel kf5-kconfig-devel
sudo dnf install kf5-kdbusaddons-devel kf5-ki18n-devel kf5-kiconthemes-devel
sudo dnf install kf5-kio-devel kf5-knotifications-devel qca-qt5-devel

daemon_management.description: KDE Connect daemon typically runs via autostart, not as a system service

custom_systemd_user_service.description: Create custom user service for better management
custom_systemd_user_service.content:
[Unit]
Description=KDE Connect Daemon for Device Communication
Documentation=man:kdeconnect(1) https://userbase.kde.org/KDEConnect

[Service]
Type=simple
ExecStart=/usr/bin/kdeconnectd
Restart=always
RestartSec=5

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target

firewalld_commands.description: Configure firewalld to allow KDE Connect traffic
gui_method: Or: Ports tab → Add 1714-1764 for both TCP and UDP

identity_packet.description: Devices broadcast identity packets with device info

benefits: Less likely to be blocked than UDP broadcast

bluetooth.pairing: Pair devices via Bluetooth settings first
bluetooth.pin_confirmation: PIN displayed on both devices for verification

manual_ip.use_case: VPN, firewalled networks, or failed auto-discovery
manual_ip.method: Add device by IP in KDE Connect settings

steps: 1. Both devices on same network or Bluetooth paired
2. Open KDE Connect on both devices
4. Initiate pairing request from either device
5. Accept pairing request on other device

process: 1. Identity packet sent over unencrypted socket
5. For paired devices: Certificate verification against stored cert

security_features.user_confirmation: Manual acceptance required for new pairings

trusted_networks.configuration: App settings to limit network access

device_trust.limitations: May not persist across reboots on some platforms

firewall_level_trust.iptables_rules: Restrict KDE Connect to specific IP ranges
firewall_level_trust.example: sudo ufw allow from 192.168.1.0/24 to any port 1714:1764

common_problems: Certificate validation failures due to outdated SSL libraries
Pairing lost after suspend or network changes
Device not detected due to firewall blocks
troubleshooting: Verify both devices on same network
Enable debug logging to identify specific errors

overview.description: DBus provides programmatic control of KDE Connect
overview.introspection: Supported for discovering methods and signals

list_devices.description: List all paired devices using Python DBus
list_devices.code:
import dbus

bus = dbus.SessionBus()
kdeconnect = bus.get_object('org.kde.kdeconnect', '/modules/kdeconnect')
iface = dbus.Interface(kdeconnect, 'org.kde.kdeconnect.daemon')
devices = iface.devices()

for device_id in devices:
    device = bus.get_object('org.kde.kdeconnect', f'/modules/kdeconnect/devices/{device_id}')
    device_iface = dbus.Interface(device, 'org.kde.kdeconnect.device')
    print(f'Device: {device_iface.name()}, ID: {device_id}, Paired: {device_iface.isPaired()}')

get_battery_level.description: Get battery level of paired phone
get_battery_level.code:
import dbus

bus = dbus.SessionBus()
kdeconnect = bus.get_object('org.kde.kdeconnect', '/modules/kdeconnect')
iface = dbus.Interface(kdeconnect, 'org.kde.kdeconnect.daemon')
devices = iface.devices()

if devices:
    device_id = devices[0]
    battery = bus.get_object('org.kde.kdeconnect', f'/modules/kdeconnect/devices/{device_id}/battery')
    battery_iface = dbus.Interface(battery, 'org.kde.kdeconnect.device.battery')
    charge = battery_iface.charge()
    charging = battery_iface.isCharging()
    print(f'Battery: {charge}%, Charging: {charging}')

send_notification.code:
import dbus

bus = dbus.SessionBus()
device_id = 'your_device_id'
notifications = bus.get_object('org.kde.kdeconnect', f'/modules/kdeconnect/devices/{device_id}/notifications')
iface = dbus.Interface(notifications, 'org.kde.kdeconnect.device.notifications')
iface.sendNotification('custom_id', 'MyApp', 'Test Notification', 'This is a test', '')

monitor_device_changes.code:
import dbus
from dbus.mainloop.glib import DBusGMainLoop
from gi.repository import GLib

DBusGMainLoop(set_as_default=True)

def device_added(device_id):
    print(f'Device added: {device_id}')

def device_removed(device_id):
    print(f'Device removed: {device_id}')

bus = dbus.SessionBus()
bus.add_signal_receiver(device_added, signal_name='deviceAdded', dbus_interface='org.kde.kdeconnect.daemon')
bus.add_signal_receiver(device_removed, signal_name='deviceRemoved', dbus_interface='org.kde.kdeconnect.daemon')

loop = GLib.MainLoop()
loop.run()

kdeconnect_cli.description: Official command-line interface to KDE Connect

native_support.protocol: KDE Connect uses custom protocol over TCP/TLS
native_support.dbus_primary: DBus is the primary real-time monitoring interface

custom_implementation.approach: Build WebSocket wrapper around DBus API

python_websocket_server.description: Example WebSocket server exposing KDE Connect via websockets
python_websocket_server.code:
import asyncio
import websockets
import dbus
import json
from dbus.mainloop.glib import DBusGMainLoop

DBusGMainLoop(set_as_default=True)
bus = dbus.SessionBus()

async def kdeconnect_handler(websocket, path):
    kdeconnect = bus.get_object('org.kde.kdeconnect', '/modules/kdeconnect')
    iface = dbus.Interface(kdeconnect, 'org.kde.kdeconnect.daemon')
    
    async for message in websocket:
        try:
            data = json.loads(message)
            action = data.get('action')
            
            if action == 'list_devices':
                devices = iface.devices()
                device_list = []
                for device_id in devices:
                    device = bus.get_object('org.kde.kdeconnect', f'/modules/kdeconnect/devices/{device_id}')
                    device_iface = dbus.Interface(device, 'org.kde.kdeconnect.device')
                    device_list.append({
                        'id': device_id,
                        'name': device_iface.name(),
                        'paired': device_iface.isPaired(),
                        'reachable': device_iface.isReachable()
                    })
                await websocket.send(json.dumps({'devices': device_list}))
            
            elif action == 'get_battery':
                device_id = data.get('device_id')
                battery = bus.get_object('org.kde.kdeconnect', f'/modules/kdeconnect/devices/{device_id}/battery')
                battery_iface = dbus.Interface(battery, 'org.kde.kdeconnect.device.battery')
                await websocket.send(json.dumps({
                    'charge': battery_iface.charge(),
                    'charging': battery_iface.isCharging()
                }))
        
        except Exception as e:
            await websocket.send(json.dumps({'error': str(e)}))

async def main():
    async with websockets.serve(kdeconnect_handler, 'localhost', 8765):
        await asyncio.Future()

asyncio.run(main())

overview.description: SSH tunneling can secure KDE Connect traffic or enable remote access
overview.limitation: KDE Connect uses UDP for discovery, SSH tunneling is TCP-based
overview.recommendation: Use OpenVPN bridged mode for full functionality

local_port_forwarding.description: Forward local port to remote KDE Connect instance
local_port_forwarding.use_case: Access KDE Connect on remote device through SSH tunnel
local_port_forwarding.limitation: Only works for already-paired devices (no UDP discovery)

remote_port_forwarding.description: Expose local KDE Connect to remote network
requirements: Restart SSH service: sudo systemctl restart sshd
remote_port_forwarding.use_case: Make local KDE Connect accessible from remote network

dynamic_port_forwarding.description: Create SOCKS proxy for flexible routing
dynamic_port_forwarding.use_case: Route all traffic through SSH tunnel
dynamic_port_forwarding.configuration: Configure applications to use SOCKS proxy at localhost:8080

bridged_mode.server_config_sample:
dev tap0
server-bridge 10.8.0.1 255.255.255.0 10.8.0.100 10.8.0.200
port 1194
proto udp
ca ca.crt
cert server.crt
key server.key
dh dh2048.pem

dev tap
remote vpn.example.com 1194
proto udp
ca ca.crt
cert client.crt
key client.key

check_listening_ports.expected_output: Should show UDP and TCP listeners on ports 1714-1764

bash_script.code:
#!/bin/bash

# KDE Connect monitoring script

LOG_FILE="/tmp/kdeconnect_monitor.log"

log_message() {
    echo "$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') - $1" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

# Check if daemon is running
if ! pgrep -x kdeconnectd > /dev/null; then
    log_message "ERROR: kdeconnectd is not running"
    log_message "Attempting to restart..."
    kdeconnectd &
    sleep 2
fi

# Check device connectivity
DEVICES=$(kdeconnect-cli --list-available --id-only)

if [ -z "$DEVICES" ]; then
    log_message "WARNING: No devices available"
else
    for DEVICE_ID in $DEVICES; do
        DEVICE_NAME=$(qdbus org.kde.kdeconnect /modules/kdeconnect/devices/$DEVICE_ID org.kde.kdeconnect.device.name)
        IS_REACHABLE=$(qdbus org.kde.kdeconnect /modules/kdeconnect/devices/$DEVICE_ID org.kde.kdeconnect.device.isReachable)
        log_message "Device: $DEVICE_NAME (ID: $DEVICE_ID) - Reachable: $IS_REACHABLE"
    done
fi

[Unit]
Description=KDE Connect monitoring timer

[Timer]
OnBootSec=5min

[Install]
WantedBy=timers.target

python_monitoring.code:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import dbus
import time
from dbus.mainloop.glib import DBusGMainLoop
from gi.repository import GLib

DBusGMainLoop(set_as_default=True)

def log_event(message):
    timestamp = time.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
    print(f'{timestamp} - {message}')
    with open('/tmp/kdeconnect_events.log', 'a') as f:
        f.write(f'{timestamp} - {message}\n')

def device_added(device_id):
    log_event(f'Device added: {device_id}')

def device_removed(device_id):
    log_event(f'Device removed: {device_id}')

def device_visibility_changed(device_id, visible):
    log_event(f'Device {device_id} visibility changed: {visible}')

def reachable_changed(reachable):
    log_event(f'Device reachability changed: {reachable}')

try:
    bus = dbus.SessionBus()
    
    # Monitor daemon signals
    bus.add_signal_receiver(
        device_added,
        signal_name='deviceAdded',
        dbus_interface='org.kde.kdeconnect.daemon',
        path='/modules/kdeconnect'
    )
    
    bus.add_signal_receiver(
        device_removed,
        signal_name='deviceRemoved',
        dbus_interface='org.kde.kdeconnect.daemon',
        path='/modules/kdeconnect'
    )
    
    bus.add_signal_receiver(
        device_visibility_changed,
        signal_name='deviceVisibilityChanged',
        dbus_interface='org.kde.kdeconnect.daemon',
        path='/modules/kdeconnect'
    )
    
    log_event('KDE Connect monitoring started')
    
    loop = GLib.MainLoop()
    loop.run()
    
except KeyboardInterrupt:
    log_event('Monitoring stopped by user')
except Exception as e:
    log_event(f'Error: {str(e)}')

diagnostic_commands.device_info: for id in $(kdeconnect-cli -a --id-only); do echo "=== Device $id ==="  && qdbus org.kde.kdeconnect /modules/kdeconnect/devices/$id org.kde.kdeconnect.device.name && qdbus org.kde.kdeconnect /modules/kdeconnect/devices/$id org.kde.kdeconnect.device.isReachable; done

tutorials.name: DBus Access Tutorial
tutorials.description: Learn to interact with DBus APIs

glossary.TAP: Network device that simulates Ethernet (Layer 2), supports broadcasts
glossary.TUN: Network device that operates at IP level (Layer 3), no broadcasts
glossary.TOFU: Trust On First Use - security model where certificates are trusted after first encounter
glossary.SFTP: Secure File Transfer Protocol - encrypted file transfer over SSH
glossary.mDNS: Multicast DNS - zero-configuration service discovery protocol
glossary.VNC: Virtual Network Computing - remote desktop protocol
